items.question: For y = [10, 12, 8] and ŷ = [9, 15, 8.5], what is the MAE?
For the same predictions, what is the RMSE (≈)?
Test MSE = 64. What is test RMSE?
Which statement is always true for the same set of residuals?
items.explanation: Square root of mean squared absolute residuals is never below mean absolute residuals; equal only when all |eᵢ| match.

items.question: Where should final RMSE/MAE be computed for an unbiased estimate?
options: Training set used to fit weights
Validation or test set not used for parameter fitting
Full dataset including train and test
items.answer: Validation or test set not used for parameter fitting
items.explanation: Held-out splits (see Train/Val/Test Splits) give generalization estimates; training metrics are optimistically biased.

items.question: MSE as a training loss is defined in which topic?
items.explanation: Loss Functions is canonical for MSE/MAE as training objectives; RMSE & MAE covers evaluation and reporting on held-out data.
